themes.name: Customer Support & Resolution
themes.description: Users report tickets being closed without resolution, lack of a contact number, and poor responsiveness from the support team.

themes.name: Transactional & Fund Reliability
themes.description: Issues with failed payments, delayed refunds, money debited but not credited to NPS/MF, and technical glitches causing negative wallet balances.

themes.name: UI/UX & Feature Regression
themes.description: Negative feedback on the new update, specifically regarding cluttered interface, large fonts, and the removal of favorite tracking features like EPF/NPS details.

themes.name: Fee Transparency & Charges
themes.description: Complaints regarding hidden DP charges, unexpected brokerage on US stocks, and discrepancies between advertised and actual MTF interest rates.

themes.name: Onboarding & KYC Complexity
themes.description: Frustration with the Federal Bank account opening process, slow KYC verification, and lack of support for NRI US stock accounts.

quotes.text: This app haven't customer care contact numbers. How to reach you with out customer care contact numbers.
The interface is very much cluttered and font size is kind of very bigger.
My previous tickets (3132454 and 3150933) were closed without any resolution.

weekly_pulse.note:
This week's feedback indicates a critical decline in user trust driven by perceived 'black-box' support and transactional failures. A recurring theme is the closure of support tickets without actual resolution, particularly concerning high-value mutual fund allotments and NPS credits. Users are threatening legal action due to these unresolved financial discrepancies. 

Product-wise, the recent UI update has caused significant friction. Users find the new interface cluttered and are specifically frustrated by the removal of granular tracking for EPF and the change in external portfolio refresh logic—moving from a simple OTP to a complex QR code process. Additionally, there is a growing sentiment of 'hidden charges' regarding DP fees and MTF rates (7.5% advertised vs 15% charged), which is driving users toward competitors like Angel One and Groww. 

To stabilize retention, we must address the reliability of the fund settlement pipeline and restore the simplified tracking features that defined the app's early value proposition. Improving support transparency by providing a clear path to human agents is essential to mitigate the 'worst app' sentiment currently trending in the reviews.
actions: Implement a 'Call Me Back' or live chat escalation feature for high-value transactional issues to address the lack of accessible support.
Revert the external mutual fund portfolio refresh logic to an OTP-based system or simplify the current QR code requirement to reduce user friction.
Conduct a pricing audit and update the UI to explicitly show DP charges and MTF rates before trade execution to improve fee transparency.

fee_scenario: Mutual fund exit load on the INDmoney platform
explanation_bullets: Exit load is a fee charged by Asset Management Companies when an investor redeems or switches out of a mutual fund scheme within a specified timeframe.
The specific percentage of the exit load and the duration of the holding period vary according to the individual mutual fund scheme's offer document.
INDmoney displays the applicable exit load details for each mutual fund scheme on the platform's fund details page.
The exit load amount is deducted from the Net Asset Value of the units being redeemed, resulting in a lower final payout to the investor.
Asset Management Companies use exit loads to manage the liquidity requirements of the fund and to account for transaction costs associated with early redemptions.
